title: conversion of string values to an interval second column is implemented incorrect and incomplete
poc: INSERT INTO INTERVAL_TBL (f1) VALUES ('1 day 2 hours 3 minutes 4 seconds');
INSERT INTO INTERVAL_TBL (f1) VALUES ('5 months 12 hours');
INSERT INTO INTERVAL_TBL (f1) VALUES ('@ 14 seconds ago');
INSERT INTO INTERVAL_TBL (f1) VALUES ('badly formatted interval');
INSERT INTO INTERVAL_TBL (f1) VALUES ('@ 30 eons ago');
SELECT cast('01:00' as interval second) AS "One hour";
SELECT cast('+02:00' as interval second) AS "Two hours";
SELECT cast('-08:00' as interval second) AS "Eight hours";
SELECT cast('-05' as interval second) AS "Five hours";
SELECT cast('-1 +02:03' as interval second) AS "22 hours ago...";
SELECT cast('-1 days +02:03' as interval second) AS "22 hours ago...";
SELECT cast('10 years -11 month -12 days +13:14' as interval second) AS "9 years...";
select * from sys.types where sqlname like '%interval%';
key_bug_features: Incorrect conversion of string values to interval second
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE INTERVAL_TBL_2 (f1 interval second NOT NULL, f2 varchar(255));
INSERT INTO INTERVAL_TBL_2 (f1, f2) VALUES ('2 days 3 hours', 'valid interval');
INSERT INTO INTERVAL_TBL_2 (f1, f2) VALUES ('@ 1 year', 'another valid interval');
INSERT INTO INTERVAL_TBL_2 (f1, f2) VALUES ('bad format', 'invalid interval');
SELECT f1, f2 FROM INTERVAL_TBL_2 WHERE f1 IS NOT NULL;
